AI Chief Of Staff

AI Emaily is the AI-native inbox that runs like your chief of staff. It reads every message, triages what actually needs you, and quiets the noise. It drafts replies in your own voice — not generic AI text — then schedules and sends across Gmail, Outlook, and any provider from one inbox. Three modes: Manual, Copilot, Autopilot. You approve, it acts, with undo and a full audit trail on everything. Your mail is never used to train models. Start free.

Free [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋 I'm Nafiul, maker of AI Emaily. I built this because my inbox had quietly become my second job. Not the thinking — the typing. The same "sounds good, Tuesday works," the polite follow-ups, the triage of what actually needs me. Every "AI email" tool I tried just summarized the pile or spat out replies that sounded nothing like me. So AI Emaily is an inbox with an autonomous assistant that: • Triages — surfaces what needs you, quiets the rest • Writes in your voice — learns your tone, drafts replies that actually sound like you • Runs on autopilot — schedules and sends, with your approval • Works everywhere — Gmail, Outlook, any provider, one inbox You stay in control the whole time: three modes (Manual → Copilot → Autopilot), human approval before any send, plus undo and [...] Report

results.title: 15 Best AI Email Assistants for Productivity in 2026 Tested
results.content:
Key Features:   
  + SecureSend: State-of-the-art encryption ensures that only the intended recipient can read your message.
  + Copilot AI: An on-device assistant that summarizes threads and drafts replies without exposing data.
 Best For: Security-conscious industries (Legal, Health, Finance) where data privacy is non-negotiable.
 Pricing: Free basic version; Growth version starts from $3/mo.

### 13. Writemail.ai

The drafter

Writemail.ai is a straightforward, no-nonsense tool. You paste the email you received, select your desired tone (Professional, Funny, Angry), and it generates a reply. It is a standalone web tool rather than an integrated plugin, making it useful for one-off drafting needs. [...] ### 14. Ellie (tryellie.com)

The style mimic

Ellie is a browser extension that prides itself on learning your writing style better than others. It allows you to define "Knowledge Base" snippets so it knows about your product or company when drafting replies. It works across Gmail and Fastmail. However, you only get a limited number of replies unless you upgrade to their most expensive plan ($79/mo).

 Key Features:   
  + Style learning: Analyzes your writing patterns to produce drafts that sound authentic to you.
  + Browser extension: Works lightly in the background of Chrome or Firefox without slowing down your browser.
 Best for: PR professionals who need to maintain a highly personal voice in their communications.
 Pricing: Starts from $19/mo for 25 replies a day. [...] ### 3. Shortwave

The Gmail transformer

Founded by ex-Google engineers, Shortwave reimagines Gmail with a modern, chat-like interface. Its "Ghostwriter" AI learns your unique writing style by analyzing your sent folder, ensuring drafts actually sound like you. It groups threads intelligently to keep your inbox tidy. However, AI usage is limited and you have to pay $100/mo for “6x more AI usage” (still not unlimited).

results.title: 6 Best AI Email Response Generators in 2025 [+ 4 Reasons to Try ...
results.content:
Mailmeter's AI email response generator

### 7. Fyxer

Fyxer positions itself a personalized AI assistant that focuses on learning and perfectly mimicking your unique communication style. It trains on your past email history to absorb your specific tone, vocabulary, and sign-offs, ensuring that the replies it helps you generate sound authentically like you.

Fyxer's AI response generator

Fyxer's AI response generator

Fyxer expands its capabilities beyond just drafting text by also offering Smart Triage to automatically sort and prioritize your inbox. Plus, it functions as an AI calendar assistant that can manage your schedule, booking and rescheduling meetings for you simply by being CC'ed on an email thread, aiming to handle the complete email lifecycle.

results.title: 10 Best AI Email Writers in 2026: From Draft to Send, We Tested ...
results.content:
‍

## The 10 Best AI Email Writers in 2026

‍

### 1. Sai by Simular - Best AI Email Writer for End-to-End Inbox Automation

Pricing: $20/month Platform: Windows (macOS coming soon) Our rating: 9.1/10

Sai by Simular is the only AI email writer we tested that works directly inside your email client. Instead of generating text in a separate app and asking you to copy-paste, Sai opens your Gmail or Outlook, reads the email thread, composes a contextual reply matching your writing style, and waits for your approval before sending. The entire workflow happens in your actual inbox. [...] #### Best for:

Marketing teams who manage email campaigns and need every message to match a specific brand voice and style.

‍

### 7. Shortwave - Best AI Email Writer for Gmail Power Users

Pricing: Free (basic) | $7/month (Pro) | $14/month (Business) Platform: Web, Mac, iOS, Android (Gmail only) Our rating: 7.9/10

Shortwave is an AI-native email client built exclusively for Gmail. Unlike tools that bolt AI onto existing email, Shortwave was designed from the ground up with AI as a core feature. The "AI Write" button appears in every compose window, and the AI generates replies based on your full email history, not just the current thread.

results.title: AI Email Responder: Best Auto-Reply Tools for 2026
results.content:
Superhuman is a keyboard-first email app built around speed, and it has layered in AI that drafts replies, summarizes long threads, and learns your tone over time. It’s a polished tier-two experience - your inbox feels lighter and replies come together faster. There’s now an optional AI auto-draft mode that prepares routine replies for you to review and send on the paid plans, but it stays inside the inbox. No real-world follow-through like outbound calls or bookings, and it sits at the premium end of email-client pricing.

### 3. Shortwave

Best for: Gmail users who want an AI-native inbox. [...] Catch is an AI executive assistant that treats your inbox as something to manage, not a place to leave suggestions lying around. It connects to Gmail or Outlook, learns from your real history, then triages incoming mail by priority - surfacing only the messages that genuinely need you and handling the routine ones itself. For replies that don’t require a decision, it responds on your behalf. When a reply does need your call, it comes to you with the context, captures your answer, and then sends. That’s the line we care about: moving from drafting to actually responding. [...] Best for: Gmail users who want an AI-native inbox.

Shortwave rebuilds the Gmail experience around an AI assistant that can search your mail, summarize threads, and draft contextual replies. It leans harder into “ask the assistant to do things” than a standard client, which makes it a strong tier-two pick. It’s Gmail-only, though, so Outlook shops are out, and the heavier AI features sit on the paid plans.

### 4. Microsoft Copilot for Outlook

Best for: organizations already standardized on Microsoft 365.
